intro: Celsia mauritanica, also known as Moroccan Celery, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for cardiovascular health and immune system function. Celsia mauritanica contains bioactive constituents such as essential oils, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, digestive disorders, and respiratory issues.
